title: The Warning of Shuǐ Guó
author: Big MAQ Studio
description: A large-scale interactive fantasy novel about Shien Yu, a village messenger who sets out to warn Shuǐ Guó of famine and uncovers a kingdom built on rewritten history, family betrayal, and an ancient hunger beneath the throne.

scenes.title: Night of the Black Water
scenes.body: In the fishing village of Yang Zing River, Shien Yu wakes from a dream in which the kingdom's canals run black, rice fields crack like old pottery, and a throne of wet stone breathes beneath the earth. The vision leaves the taste of silt in his mouth and the certainty that if he stays quiet, Shuǐ Guó will starve. Before dawn fully breaks, he must decide how to begin the warning that may save a kingdom or shatter it.
scenes.bonusText: In the dream, one detail burns brighter than the rest: a bell ringing in a city Shien has never seen.

choices.label: Wake the village elder immediately
Go first to the river shrine before sunrise
Seek Li Wei at the abandoned watchfire
Hire Bo Ren's ferry before the market wakes

scenes.title: The Elder's Reed Maps
scenes.body: Elder Suyin listens without laughing, which frightens Shien more than disbelief would have. She unrolls reed maps of trade roads, shrine lines, and the old flood channels, then admits that the harvest accounts from Yung Shao arrived lighter than they should have. If the famine has already begun, the warning cannot wait.

choices.label: Take the elder's horn to the river shrine
Ride straight for Yung Shao with the maps
Ask Li Wei to become your sword arm
Seek Bo Ren's ferry and the river routes

scenes.title: Shrine of the First Current
scenes.body: Mist coils around the old shrine as if the river itself were listening. Broken tablets speak of Water Guardians, covenant bells, and a hunger that rises whenever rulers store grain in secret while the poor go empty. Shien senses the place is trying to warn him, but in Shuǐ Guó every warning comes with a price.
scenes.bonusText: One cracked line on the tablets mentions a guardian that turned on its makers when vows were broken.

choices.label: Offer rice and ring the shrine bell
Read every cracked tablet before moving
Leave at once for Yung Shao
Follow the black water running north

scenes.title: The River Serpent of Yang Zing
scenes.body: At the ford beyond the shrine, the river erupts and a serpent plated in mud-dark scales coils around toppled ferry posts. Villagers once left it offerings, but now it tears nets apart as if enraged by a vow broken far upstream. Defeating it is possible; understanding why it rose may matter more.

choices.label: Harpoon the serpent in open water
Drive it back with the shrine bell and horn
Calm it with offerings and the old rite
Follow it into the reeds to see what it guards

scenes.title: Li Wei Beneath the Burned Banner
scenes.body: Li Wei, once an imperial guard, keeps a lonely camp under a weather-ruined banner he no longer claims. He hears the word famine, sharpens his spear without a word, and asks Shien whether this journey is meant to save the kingdom or expose it. It is the first time Shien realizes those may become different goals.

choices.label: Recruit Li Wei openly
Ask him about imperial roads and garrisons
Leave before dawn with only his warning
Let him shadow you from a distance

scenes.title: Bo Ren and the Hidden Channels
scenes.body: Bo Ren smiles like a gambler who has already stacked the table in his favor. He knows the river tolls, the smugglers' bends, and which warehouses claim flood damage while quietly shipping grain to the coast. If Shien travels with him, speed will not be the only thing gained.

choices.label: Pay Bo Ren for the fastest passage
Ask him what trade routes look wrong
Cross alone and keep your coin
Take the hidden reed channels with him
choices.effects: xp += 10; boRen = true; allies += 1; mapFound = true

scenes.title: Yung Shao's Hungry Gates
scenes.body: The first city Shien reaches already looks thinner than memory: fewer fish on hooks, shorter lines of firewood, longer lines outside the granaries. Gate scribes speak of 'temporary shortage' while soldiers inspect carts heavy enough to prove otherwise. The famine is no rumor now; it has a bureaucratic face.

choices.label: Enter through the merchant arch
Chase grain thieves over the wall
Read the tax notices and ration decrees
Take the northern road to Jade Pass

scenes.title: A Market with Full Ledgers and Empty Bowls
scenes.body: Yung Shao's market still clatters, but the sound is wrong. Merchants argue over luxury spices while children wait outside noodle stalls that have nothing left to sell. Bo Ren calls it the smell of a city lying to itself.

choices.label: Feed children from your travel pack
Trace the stolen carts to their handlers
Meet guild scribes in a shuttered tea house
Leave before the city swallows time and food alike

scenes.title: The Bandit Lord's Granary Fortress
scenes.body: Shien discovers that the 'bandits' outside Yung Shao are less a rabble than a supply chain with swords. Their lord keeps stolen grain in a canyon storehouse stamped with seals from noble houses that deny any connection. Fighting him may clear the road; exposing him may change far more.

choices.label: Duel the Bandit Lord in the rain
Expose his noble employer to his own men
choices.effects: xp += 20; ledgerFound = true; truths += 1; thirdMarks += 1; peopleTrust += 1

choices.label: Free the prisoners while the gang splinters
Offer surrender if every grain sack is returned

scenes.title: The Ledgers No Official Wanted Read
scenes.body: Behind false walls and false taxes, Shien finds ledgers proving that grain moved out of Yung Shao even as the city was ordered to tighten rations. Numbers become names, names become houses, and the famine begins to look less like disaster than design. The road to Jade Pass now carries more than warning; it carries evidence.

choices.label: Read every page by lantern light
Give copies to Bo Ren for safekeeping
Send proof ahead to the capital
Hide the proof until you understand the whole pattern

scenes.title: Jade Pass and the Wind of Old Vows
scenes.body: The mountains above Shuǐ Guó feel older than the empire and less willing to forgive it. At Jade Pass, broken guardian statues stare over switchback roads that once carried tribute, prisoners, and flood engineers. Li Wei mutters that armies marched here in the dark years and came back speaking less than they knew.

choices.label: Climb the guardian stairs
March through the soldier tunnel
Follow runoff into a forgotten cave
Rest at Li Wei's old checkpoint

scenes.title: Trial of Memory, Duty, and Mercy
scenes.body: Three carved doors stand in the cliffs: one marked by tears, one by spears, and one by open hands. The guardian system of old Shuǐ Guó did not merely defend roads; it judged the people who used them. Shien must answer which kind of ruler the kingdom deserves before the mountain will answer back.

choices.label: Choose the trial of memory
Choose the trial of duty
Choose the trial of mercy
Break the seals and rush onward

scenes.title: The Stone Guardian of Jade Pass
scenes.body: The mountain wakes in the shape of a warrior larger than a house, its chest glowing with cold trapped lightning. It asks no question out loud, yet every blow it delivers feels like one: what is Shien willing to destroy to make himself heard? To pass Jade Pass is to pass under judgment.

choices.label: Shatter the guardian with force
Cool its core with shrine water
Speak the vow hidden in the tablets
Let Li Wei draw its attention while you pass

scenes.title: The Memory Buried Inside Stone
scenes.body: When the guardian breaks or kneels, its last gift is memory. Shien sees flood engineers under imperial banners forcing guardians to turn their strength toward ration vaults and river locks, not toward the people they were sworn to defend. The famine suddenly has ancestors.
scenes.bonusText: One flicker of the memory shows a jade seal in the hand of an emperor watching starving farmers from a dry balcony.

choices.label: Accept that the guardians hid terrible truths
Reject the memory as a mountain trick
Ask where the black water flowed next
Carry the broken jade seal as evidence

scenes.title: Nanhai, City of Salt and Shortage
scenes.body: Nanhai should smell of wealth and sea wind, but the harbor reeks of rotten rope, panic, and hidden cargo. Grain ships sit under armed guard while fishing families trade heirlooms for half-sacks of millet. The coast has not escaped the famine; it has become one of its engines.

choices.label: Help unload boats for starving villages
Track the pirate fleet through the harbor
Follow whispers to a healer's hidden clinic
Search the tax storehouses first

scenes.title: Harbor of the Tide-Crowned Pirates
scenes.body: The pirates of Nanhai are not random raiders; they strike only the ships that would have fed inland villages. Someone pays them to do it, and someone richer profits from every empty bowl upriver. If Shien reaches their king, he may find a criminal or a witness wearing another man's mask.

choices.label: Board the flagship under moonlight
Rally dockworkers into a diversion
Ask Bo Ren for the smugglers' back routes
Hunt the assassin rumored to serve the fleet

scenes.title: The Pirate King of Nanhai
scenes.body: The Pirate King waits on a deck draped in prayer flags stolen from drowned shrines, laughing like the sea itself taught him cruelty. Yet his captains watch the harbor storehouses, not the open horizon, as if their orders come from land. Defeating him may break a fleet; hearing him out may crack the conspiracy wider open.

choices.label: Fight blade to blade on the mast
Cut the chain holding the grain barges
Offer amnesty in exchange for testimony
Burn the flagship and seize every chart

scenes.title: Mei Lin's Lantern Clinic
scenes.body: Hidden between shuttered inns, Mei Lin runs a clinic lit by fish-oil lamps and stubbornness. She treats famine fever, catalogues unusual blight patterns, and has already concluded that the rot moving through Shuǐ Guó behaves like policy before it behaves like plague. She does not ask whether Shien is brave; she asks whether he is willing to learn.

choices.label: Ask Mei Lin to join your journey
Help treat the fever tents before leaving
Compare her notes with your ledgers
Leave medicine and head for the warehouses

scenes.title: Warehouses of White Salt and Dark Grain
scenes.body: The warehouse district is guarded more tightly than a palace chapel. Salt barrels line the outer rooms, but behind them lie sealed grain holds marked for nonexistent flood relief programs. The longer Shien looks, the clearer it becomes that someone has practiced this theft for years.

choices.label: Confront the noble factor hiding the grain
Mark the crates and follow them secretly
Free the workers locked in the storehouse
Take the evidence straight to Mei Lin

scenes.title: The Mist Assassin
scenes.body: Shien's enemy comes wrapped in harbor fog and silk-thin knives, moving with the precision of someone trained by people who erase witnesses for a living. Mid-fight, the assassin speaks a name Shien never expected to hear in Nanhai: General Xuan. Whether it is truth, bait, or both depends on what Shien is willing to risk.

choices.label: Fight the assassin through the rain garden
Corner the assassin and demand the full story
Spare the assassin for a hidden route map
Promise to reveal the conspiracy if the assassin yields

scenes.title: Temple of Tides
scenes.body: Nanhai's oldest sea temple has been left to salt and gulls, yet the flood murals remain vivid enough to accuse the living. Mei Lin points out that the carvings do not portray famine as nature's anger, but as the result of gates, channels, and deliberate redirection. Whoever controls the water writes the history that follows.
scenes.bonusText: One mural shows a man in imperial robes offering grain to an unseen mouth beneath the floor.

choices.label: Study the flood murals with Mei Lin
Kneel before the salt-worn altar
Search the drowned crypts below
Leave offerings for the dead sailors

scenes.title: Map of Hidden Water and Hidden Power
scenes.body: Whether taken from smugglers, pirates, or assassins, the map tells the same ugly truth: food disappears where record books say it should flow, then resurfaces where powerful men prefer it counted. It also marks routes to Yu Shu, drowned crypts, and a sanctuary that no public survey admits exists. The conspiracy is geographic now.

choices.label: Follow the map inland toward Yu Shu
Visit the hidden granary beneath the cliffs
Copy every route before moving on
Burn the map so no tyrant can claim it

scenes.title: Convoy of Banned Questions
scenes.body: On the north road, Shien encounters scholars traveling under quiet guard to Yu Shu. Their chests contain censored census rolls, old shrine receipts, and letters from magistrates whose final lines all stop in the middle of blame. The further north the convoy goes, the less accidental the famine feels.

choices.label: Escort the scholars to Yu Shu
Read their sealed letters on the road
Ask about erased dynasties
Keep watch while the convoy sleeps

scenes.title: Yu Shu, City of Bells and Censors
scenes.body: Yu Shu rises out of cold rain like a carved answer waiting for the right question. Monasteries cling to the heights, scholars guard lower archives, and everywhere bells sound as if the city wants to remember something the empire ordered it to forget. Shien enters a city built on learning and fear in equal measure.

choices.label: Enter by the scholar bridge
Climb to the monastery bells
Follow the monk who spoke your family name
Search for the drowned road beneath the city

scenes.title: The Great Library's Missing Shelves
scenes.body: The Great Library of Yu Shu is vast enough to humble kings, which may be why kings have spent centuries trimming it. Entire shelf lines have been removed and replaced with polished stone, as if absence itself were an acceptable form of recordkeeping. To study here is to learn from what survived and from what was deliberately erased.

choices.label: Read the famine annals
Search for records on Yu Ren
Ask about the Water Guardians
Steal a censored scroll before the scribes notice

scenes.title: Archive of Wet Ink and Dry Lies
scenes.body: Hidden behind false walls, the archive keeps duplicate histories: one for the people, one for those who govern them. Shien finds references to engineered shortages, vanished flood brigades, and a figure called the Black Flood who was condemned in public but petitioned in secret. From this point on, ignorance becomes a choice.

choices.label: Open the sealed emperor edict
Compare altered histories side by side
Share what you found with Mei Lin
Hide the scroll and seek the monks at once

scenes.title: Steps of the Quiet Monastery
scenes.body: The monastery above Yu Shu teaches stillness, but its novices move like men guarding powder stores. Here Shien learns that monks preserved prophecies in fragments so the empire could not destroy them all at once. One fragment mentions a son of the river who would stand between throne, flood, and dragon.

choices.label: Debate doctrine with the abbots
Ring the bell that wakes old memories
Ask for shelter, prophecy, and a guide
Refuse ceremony and head for the drowned road

scenes.title: Xiao Feng Behind a Prayer Door
scenes.body: Xiao Feng has been confined inside a vow-cell not because he sinned, but because he speaks too clearly. He knows Shien's name, his father's title, and the location of a drowned temple the empire would rather sink again than admit exists. Releasing him means inviting prophecy into the journey in a form that can answer back.

choices.label: Free Xiao Feng from the vow-cell
Ask why he knows your family name
Demand the prophecy immediately
Leave him, but take his map charm

scenes.title: The Drowned Road Below Yu Shu
scenes.body: Beneath the city, old paving stones vanish into black water and broken shrine lamps. The drowned road once connected monastery, archive, and temple, which means the empire knew those truths belonged together. Every step toward the temple feels like walking deeper into a story someone tried to drown.

choices.label: March through the flooded causeway
Search sidetunnels for relics and marks
Let Xiao Feng guide the prayer route
Return once more to the emperor records

scenes.title: The Iron Warlord of the Pumps
scenes.body: The drowned road is defended by a warlord in rusted plate fed by pressure pumps that drink from buried canals. He calls himself the keeper of balance, though his balance is measured in who drinks and who drowns. To beat him is to take the road; to understand him is to glimpse how long this machinery of scarcity has endured.

choices.label: Defeat the Iron Warlord in open combat
Break the pumps feeding his armor
Offer his soldiers water and pardon
Invoke Yu Ren's forgotten banner and duel him

scenes.title: Temple Beneath the Scholar City
scenes.body: The Drowned Temple is not abandoned. It is waiting, full of murals that show guardians, emperors, and flood engineers kneeling before a shape painted only as a mouth beneath the earth. Shien understands, perhaps for the first time, that famine in Shuǐ Guó may be a ritual wearing the mask of administration.

choices.label: Enter the central sanctum
Search the murals of the first flood
Pray at the cracked pool
Send companions to guard the exits

scenes.title: Spirit of the Drowned Temple
scenes.body: The temple's guardian spirit rises from the pool wearing chains of prayer bronze and imperial iron. It is less a monster than a witness forced to keep remembering the same betrayal. When it attacks, it does so like a being furious that history survived only in its wounds.

choices.label: Destroy the spirit and seize its knowledge
Free the spirit from the emperor's chains
Let Mei Lin read the old pact while you defend her
Swear to carry the spirit's warning to the world

scenes.title: Summons of the Black Flood
scenes.body: When Shien leaves the temple, masked riders wait in silence and offer neither threat nor comfort. They bear the insignia of the Black Flood, a name the empire taught children to fear and scholars to avoid writing. Their leader says only this: 'If you want the truth about the famine, come meet the man they buried alive in history.'

choices.label: Follow the masked envoy to their camp
Demand proof before taking another step
Ask Li Wei whether this feels like a trap
Turn back toward the generals and warn them

scenes.title: Your Father, Called the Black Flood
scenes.body: In a ruined watchhall lit by storm lanterns, Shien finally meets the figure the kingdom blamed for flood, revolt, and vanished garrisons. The man is older, harder, and far more tired than the legends, yet when he speaks Shien's childhood nickname, every rehearsed accusation collapses. Yu Ren did not die. He became a lie useful enough for rulers to keep alive.
scenes.bonusText: Yu Ren says the kingdom did not merely betray him; it needed him monstrous so no one would ask what he had tried to stop.

choices.label: Listen when Yu Ren says he is your father
choices.effects: xp += 15; fatherKnown = true; yurenTrust += 1; truths += 1; thirdMarks += 1

choices.label: Accuse him of bringing famine with him
Ask why the kingdom erased his name
Leave with the truth but not forgiveness

scenes.title: Memory of Betrayal
scenes.body: Yu Ren shows Shien the memory of a campaign the kingdom still teaches as noble victory. In the memory, engineers divert relief grain, generals defend the order, and Yu Ren breaks rank when he discovers villages starved to preserve ration panic. The Black Flood was born not from hunger for power, but from refusal to keep helping power feed on hunger.

choices.label: Believe the memory of betrayal
Believe only what records can prove
Ask what role the Water Dragon played
Ask how the famine can end without civil war

scenes.title: Edicts from the First Emperor
scenes.body: The sealed edicts are not policy notes; they are design documents for recurring crisis. The first emperor discovered that hunger made populations obedient, that flood damage justified emergency authority, and that controlled scarcity allowed the throne to appear necessary every generation. Shuǐ Guó was not merely governed through famine; famine helped build it.

choices.label: Read the ration laws line by line
Compare Xuan's orders with old decrees
Copy evidence for the people, not the court
Hide nothing and carry every record onward

scenes.title: When the Bell Calls All Sides to War
scenes.body: The great bell of Yu Shu rings not for prayer but for mobilization. General Xuan summons loyal soldiers to Ma Fong, the Black Flood calls its cells to converge, and old shrine lines awaken toward the sanctuary springs. Shien now stands at the point where one warning fractures into several possible futures.

choices.label: Answer General Xuan's summons to Ma Fong
Answer Yu Ren's call to the hidden camp
Follow the hidden routes to the Dragon Sanctuary
Refuse every banner and plan your own path
Try to call all sides to parley

scenes.title: The Oaths Shien Can Still Choose
scenes.body: Away from bell towers and commanders, Shien takes stock of what he knows and what he still refuses to become. Every road ahead asks for allegiance, but oaths taken too early tend to become cages. Before leaving Yu Shu, he can choose an ideal before he chooses a faction.

choices.label: Swear to protect the realm from collapse
Swear to break the corrupt order
Swear to restore balance between river and throne
Swear to trust no ruler until the full truth is known
Swear to reconcile blood, kingdom, and dragon

scenes.title: Roads Converging on Ma Fong
scenes.body: By the time Shien descends from Yu Shu, every road leads toward Ma Fong, the military heart of Shuǐ Guó. Refugees, scouts, rebels, and quartermasters all move in the same direction for different reasons. It feels less like marching toward a city than toward a verdict.
scenes.chapter: Chapter 4 - The Fall of the Throne

choices.label: Ride with refugees to the military capital
Slip into Yu Ren's lines before the siege
Detour to the sanctuary springs
Use Bo Ren's tunnels beneath Ma Fong

scenes.title: Li Wei's Confession
scenes.body: At last Li Wei admits what his silence has protected: he once served in campaigns that kept suspiciously close count of hunger, loyalty, and flood losses. He did not build the system, but he benefited from its order until the numbers became faces he could not stop seeing. His confession offers Shien a choice between anger and use, between blame and repair.
scenes.chapter: Chapter 4 - The Fall of the Throne

choices.label: Forgive Li Wei and keep him close
Demand he help expose General Xuan
Send him to Yu Ren as living witness
Tell him to leave before more blood is spilled

scenes.title: The Council of Ma Fong
scenes.body: Ma Fong reeks of lamp oil, horse sweat, and ration fear. Inside its war halls, generals debate supply lines while pretending the starving villages beyond their walls are unfortunate arithmetic. General Xuan is not blind, but whether he is trapped, complicit, or both remains for Shien to discover.
scenes.chapter: Chapter 4 - The Fall of the Throne

choices.label: Warn Xuan that famine will break the army first
Demand the granaries be opened to the poor
Reveal evidence of noble profiteering
Spy on the council and keep your own counsel

scenes.title: General Xuan at Last
scenes.body: General Xuan receives Shien without ceremony, as though he is too tired for theater and too practiced to show it. He admits the capital withheld news, denies engineering famine, and refuses to let Shuǐ Guó collapse into civil war while enemies circle. Whether he is a final shield against chaos or a polished face on an old machine depends on what Shien asks him to become.
scenes.chapter: Chapter 4 - The Fall of the Throne

choices.label: Stand with Xuan to preserve order
Challenge Xuan to admit the throne lied
Ask Xuan to meet Yu Ren under truce
Leave the court and seek the Dragon Sanctuary

scenes.title: Camp of the Black Flood
scenes.body: Yu Ren's camp is disciplined, underfed, and more humane than the kingdom's propaganda ever allowed. Here Shien sees rebels repairing wells, rationing fairly, and preparing for war they believe cannot be avoided. Revolution looks noble around cookfires and much harsher on the maps spread between commanders.
scenes.chapter: Chapter 4 - The Fall of the Throne

choices.label: Swear yourself to Yu Ren's revolt
Demand the rebels spare civilians above all else
Steal the war maps to keep options open
Beg Yu Ren to meet Xuan in peace

scenes.title: Approach to the Dragon Sanctuary
scenes.body: The sanctuary lies where public roads refuse to map the world correctly. Water climbs uphill in silver threads, reeds whisper in no wind, and carved markers warn that the dragon judges intent as quickly as trespass. Nature in this place is not innocent, but it is honest.
scenes.chapter: Chapter 4 - The Fall of the Throne

choices.label: Enter the flooded canyon alone
Let Xiao Feng read the sanctuary vows
Offer the river blessing at the gate
Search the outer shrine network first

scenes.title: Network of Forgotten Shrines
scenes.body: Three linked shrines ring the sanctuary like locks around an older promise. Each contains evidence that guardians, monks, and river wardens once cooperated to restrain something below the throne, then slowly stopped speaking of it in the same language. Repairing these shrines may not end the war, but it may change what survives it.
scenes.chapter: Chapter 4 - The Fall of the Throne

choices.label: Rekindle all three shrine braziers
Read the drowned oaths of the guardians
Send companions to spread food caches nearby
Use the shrines to summon all factions to one place

scenes.title: Planning the Third Path
scenes.body: Shien steps away from crowns, banners, and bloodline claims long enough to ask the question everyone else treats as weakness: what if every side is partly right and catastrophically incomplete? Bo Ren offers routes, Mei Lin offers evidence, Li Wei offers logistics, and Xiao Feng offers prophecy. The third path is not peace by wish; it is strategy against momentum.
scenes.chapter: Chapter 4 - The Fall of the Throne

choices.label: Infiltrate Ma Fong archives with Bo Ren
Gather testimony from refugees and soldiers
Ask Mei Lin to trace the blight to the catacombs
Prepare a parley between Xuan and Yu Ren

scenes.title: South Breach of Ma Fong
scenes.body: War arrives with drums and ration carts in equal measure. Shien fights for the kingdom route beneath Ma Fong's walls while hearing the rebels cry for justice from the other side. Even the most ordered line of spears cannot hide that every soldier in this battle was fed by a system that starved someone else.
scenes.chapter: Chapter 4 - The Fall of the Throne

choices.label: Lead the shield wall at the south breach
Evacuate civilians before the assault
Hunt the general directing the Black Flood vanguard
Break away and search the catacombs beneath the fort

scenes.title: The Rebel Push Through Ma Fong
scenes.body: On the Black Flood route, Shien learns that righteous revolt still smells like smoke, blood, and snapped prayer ropes. Yu Ren's people strike granaries, engine towers, and guard posts with disciplined fury, but even just wars can become hungry wars if no one reins them in. The rebellion needs a conscience as badly as it needs victory.
scenes.chapter: Chapter 4 - The Fall of the Throne

choices.label: Seize the granaries for the starving
Strike directly at Xuan's war engines
Duel the kingdom champion at the causeway
Slip away to learn what even Yu Ren does not know

scenes.title: Trial Before the Water Dragon
scenes.body: The Water Dragon is vast, ancient, and less interested in human innocence than human patterns. It shows Shien rivers choked by dams, villages abandoned for quotas, and forests cut to feed war machines that never seem full. To earn the dragon's aid, Shien must prove he wants more than a new master sitting in an old place.
scenes.chapter: Chapter 4 - The Fall of the Throne

choices.label: Accept the trial of memory
Accept the trial of sacrifice
Beg the dragon to spare the innocent cities
Ask the dragon about the first emperor

scenes.title: Vault Heist Under Ma Fong
scenes.body: The independent route takes Shien through cisterns, archive shafts, and smugglers' vents beneath the military capital. It is not as glorious as war, but perhaps more honest: in hidden rooms, one can see the ledgers and seals that actually move kingdoms. Here truth is not debated; it is stored in numbered boxes.
scenes.chapter: Chapter 4 - The Fall of the Throne

choices.label: Steal the archive key from the vault
Slip through the cistern into the catacombs
Publish the stolen evidence across the camps
Retreat to the last shrine and prepare a third path

scenes.title: Conclave of River, Sword, and Blood
scenes.body: Against all reasonable odds, Shien gathers rivals who expected to meet only on battlefields. Xuan arrives armored but weary, Yu Ren arrives armed but listening, and the sanctuary witnesses refuse to bless a lie for either side. The conclave does not erase history; it forces history into the same room.
scenes.chapter: Chapter 4 - The Fall of the Throne

choices.label: Bring Xuan, Yu Ren, and Mei Lin into one chamber
Ask Xiao Feng to witness a binding oath
Demand every faction descend together into the catacombs
Seek the last shrine before any throne is touched

scenes.title: Entry to the Capital Catacombs
scenes.body: Beneath the conflict above lies the older architecture of control: dry stairs for emperors, flooded channels for engineers, iron ladders for those who cleaned the system and never appeared in its histories. The catacombs smell like wet grain and old fear. Shien is finally close enough to hear the thing beneath the throne breathing.

choices.label: Follow the dry imperial stairs
Follow the flooded channel beneath the throne
Ask companions to hold the exit while you descend
Search for the heartbeat of the blight

scenes.title: Record of the First Emperor
scenes.body: At the root of the stairs, Shien finds the oldest surviving confession in Shuǐ Guó: the first emperor discovered an ancient entity beneath the capital and learned that fear, scarcity, and obedient relief would keep both throne and monster fed. Every cycle of famine reinforced imperial necessity. The kingdom's greatest stability was built on a buried appetite.
scenes.bonusText: The record uses one title over and over for the entity below: the Devourer of Harvests.

choices.label: Read the record aloud to your companions
choices.effects: xp += 18; truths += 1; emperorTruth = true; devourerTruth = true

choices.label: Copy the record and hide it from kings and rebels alike
Burn the record and trust only living testimony
Trace the ration sigils deeper underground

scenes.title: General of the Black Flood
scenes.body: In one route, the final military obstacle is a legendary commander whose loyalty has curdled into fanatic certainty. He believes the only way to stop cycles of hunger is to seize total control long enough to starve the machine instead. Shien sees in him the danger of becoming right about the system and wrong about everything else.

choices.label: Defeat the general and spare the survivors
Break the general's banner and leave no witness
Convince the general the throne and revolt are both being used
Let Li Wei challenge him while you reach the deeper vaults

scenes.title: The Corrupted Water Dragon
scenes.body: What the sanctuary hid was not a perfect guardian, but a guardian slowly poisoned by centuries of imperial siphoning. The Water Dragon now thrashes between wrath and memory, vast enough to drown a province and wounded enough to beg for release in the language of storms. Shien must choose whether to kill a symbol or save a truth.

choices.label: Purify the dragon with shrine water
Slay the dragon and claim its fearsome strength
Endure the dragon's memories until it recognizes the old pact
Let Xiao Feng chant the lost sutra while you defend him

scenes.title: Hall of Masks
scenes.body: The flooded branch of the catacombs leads through a hall where masks of emperors, rebels, generals, guardians, and peasants hang in endless rows. Some show Shien a future version of himself wearing them. The hall's lesson is cruel and useful: every righteous story can harden into a mask once it wants power to stop listening.

choices.label: Face the mask showing you as a tyrant
Face the mask showing your father's fall
Face the mask showing the kingdom starving forever
Smash every mask and keep walking

scenes.title: Throne of Tides
scenes.body: The old throne chamber is partly natural cave, partly imperial architecture, and entirely blasphemy against common sense. Channels in the floor carry water, blood, and grain dust toward a sealed well beneath the throne. Here Shien finally sees that kingship in Shuǐ Guó was engineered around feeding something hidden rather than serving anyone living.

choices.label: Touch the throne and feel the old engine waking
Reject the throne and search below it
Call Xuan or Yu Ren to witness what you found
Listen for the devourer's breath below stone

scenes.title: Antechamber of the Devourer
scenes.body: The antechamber walls are glazed with old grain offerings fused by moisture and time. Every dynasty added new locks, new slogans, and new excuses, but none stopped feeding the hollow below because none wanted to lose the authority crisis gave them. The Devourer of Harvests was never simply hidden; it was institutionalized.

choices.label: Break the locks feeding the ancient chamber
Search the side crypts for one last shrine key
Offer blood to see the final truth clearly
Pull your companions close and swear no one rules alone again

scenes.title: The Last Shrine Beneath the Capital
scenes.body: Hidden off the antechamber lies a shrine no public history admits exists. Its basin is fed by the same underground water that once sustained the capital before emperors diverted it toward control. If relit, the shrine might weaken the cycle enough for a different ending to become possible.

choices.label: Rekindle the last shrine flame
Ask the river spirits to witness your choice
Let each companion hold one corner of the rite
Carry the shrine key back without using it

scenes.title: The Devourer of Harvests
scenes.body: At the bottom of the capital's oldest shaft, Shien finally faces the thing every famine cycle served. The Devourer is not a beast in the simple sense; it is a root-mass of mouths, memory, and need, swollen by centuries of fear administered as policy. To kill it is one task. To decide what replaces the system that fed it is the harder one.

choices.label: Strike at the devourer with kingdom steel
Cut its heart with revolt fire and bitter truth
Chain it with dragon water and root-song
Study its hunger and refuse every faction's claim
Unite oath, blood, and shrine flame against it

scenes.title: After the Throne - Kingdom Route
scenes.body: The Devourer is broken, but the kingdom remains. Granaries, commands, courts, and roads still need someone to direct them, and every surviving noble suddenly remembers how much they love order. On this route, Shien must decide what kind of order deserves to survive.

choices.label: Rebuild with mercy and open granaries
Serve as shield of the restored court
Accept command of the army in troubled times
Sit upon the hidden throne yourself

scenes.title: After the Throne - Black Flood Route
scenes.body: The old system lies in pieces and the rebels stand where officials once stood. Revolution has won the room, but not yet the future. Shien must decide whether upheaval becomes reform, justice, or a new appetite wearing sharper ideals.

choices.label: Stand beside Yu Ren and reform the realm
Turn the rebellion into a people's assembly
Take the flood banner for yourself
Burn every throne to break the cycle brutally

scenes.title: After the Throne - Dragon Route
scenes.body: Nature has a voice in Shuǐ Guó again, and it is older than every crown above the capital. The dragon remembers every broken river law and every drowned village oath. Shien must decide whether balance means coexistence, retreat, vengeance, or devotion.

choices.label: Forge a covenant between city and river
Let forests reclaim the ruined frontier
Ride with the dragon beyond the eastern sea
Remain at the sanctuary as its eternal keeper

scenes.title: After the Throne - Independent Route
scenes.body: No faction owns the ending, which means none can claim the full legitimacy of victory. Shien possesses truth, surviving witnesses, and enough scars to distrust any simple answer. On the independent route, he must decide whether his power is best used by leaving, writing, building, or guarding.

choices.label: Leave Shuǐ Guó and carry its warning abroad
Stay and write the true history
Build a trade league that feeds the hungry first
Guard the catacombs where the old machine slept

scenes.title: After the Throne - True Route
scenes.body: On the true route, Shien has done the hardest thing in Shuǐ Guó: he has created a future in which no single old story gets to pretend it was enough. Peace, reform, memory, and sacrifice now depend on what covenant he asks the survivors to keep. A kingdom can be saved and still be unworthy unless rebuilt differently.

choices.label: Forge peace between Xuan and Yu Ren
Become guardian, never ruler
Carry the truth to neighboring kingdoms
Create a new covenant of river and people

scenes.title: Ending - Hero of the Rivers
scenes.body: Shien forces the granaries open, publishes enough truth to shame the surviving court, and spends the first harvest season riding from canal town to canal town making sure relief truly arrives. He becomes beloved not because he sat the throne, but because he never did. In Shuǐ Guó, that restraint becomes its own kind of legend.

scenes.title: Ending - Kingdom Protector
scenes.body: By trusting Xuan just enough and forcing him to trust in return, Shien helps preserve Shuǐ Guó without preserving every lie that built it. He becomes the protector of a wounded state whose survival depends on uncomfortable reform. It is not the cleanest ending, but it spares the most fragile lives in the short term.

scenes.title: Ending - Military Regent
scenes.body: Experience, crisis, and necessity push Shien into command. He rules not as emperor but as regent over a state too unstable for ceremony, too frightened for idealism, and too exhausted to resist him. Peace returns, but it wears armor, and everyone knows armor can forget when to come off.

scenes.title: Ending - Hidden Emperor
scenes.body: Knowing how the old machine worked, Shien chooses to claim it rather than destroy it fully. He tells himself he will use secrecy to prevent worse tyrants from rising, but secrecy has always said that. The famine cycles end for a time, yet Shuǐ Guó learns too late that a hidden emperor still counts as an emperor.

scenes.title: Ending - Black Flood Heir
scenes.body: Shien stands beside Yu Ren as the old order falls and helps transform rebellion into the foundation of a new state. Father and child do not erase their years apart, but they turn shared pain into a disciplined reconstruction of the realm. The Black Flood becomes not a terror, but a remembered purge that made room for justice.

scenes.title: Ending - Revolution of Rain
scenes.body: Instead of trading one command structure for another, Shien and the rebels turn local councils, ration boards, and village voices into the new architecture of Shuǐ Guó. It is messier, slower, and constantly threatened by opportunists, but famine can no longer hide behind distant seals. Rain still falls unevenly, yet power no longer does.

scenes.title: Ending - Tyrant of the Dikes
scenes.body: Shien takes the flood banner, defeats every rival, and decides hunger can never rule the kingdom again because fear will rule first. Roads become efficient, theft becomes rare, and dissent disappears into labor camps beside the canals. The famine ends, but Shuǐ Guó learns that efficiency without mercy is only a cleaner form of ruin.

scenes.title: Ending - Destroyer of Thrones
scenes.body: Shien concludes that every throne in Shuǐ Guó is too stained to survive. Palaces burn, archives scatter, and the old capital is reduced to cautionary stone. The people are free of dynastic control, but also of the institutions that once coordinated river law, which leaves the future open and terrifying in equal measure.

scenes.title: Ending - Dragon Compact
scenes.body: Rather than enthrone nature over humanity or humanity over nature, Shien forces a covenant both can enforce. Rivers regain legal personhood, floodworks require guardian rites, and towns are rebuilt in cooperation with the sanctuary. It is the first arrangement in generations that treats balance as relationship rather than domination.

scenes.title: Ending - Nature Reclaimed
scenes.body: Shien sides with the dragon hard enough that frontier towns are abandoned and old canal districts drown under deliberate restoration. Forests return, estuaries heal, and the land finally breathes free of imperial extraction. Humanity survives, but only where it learns humility quickly enough.

scenes.title: Ending - Dragon Rider
scenes.body: By earning the dragon's full trust, Shien leaves Shuǐ Guó not as ruler but as witness on the wing of myth. Together they travel the coastlines and inland rivers beyond the kingdom, warning other lands what famine and power can become when left married for too long. Shuǐ Guó loses a hero and gains a legend with a moving horizon.

scenes.title: Ending - Eternal Watcher
scenes.body: Shien chooses to remain at the sanctuary and monitor the old systems forever. He becomes a living warning more than a public figure, part monk, part guardian, part self-chosen exile. Shuǐ Guó stabilizes, but he pays for that stability by stepping outside ordinary life altogether.

scenes.title: Ending - Wanderer of Salt Roads
scenes.body: With the crisis ended but no faction convincing enough to deserve him, Shien leaves Shuǐ Guó by trade road and fishing coast. He becomes the one who arrives in troubled places carrying a story others would rather call impossible. Some kingdoms welcome him. Others close their gates and later regret it.

scenes.title: Ending - Scholar of Tides
scenes.body: Shien remains in Shuǐ Guó long enough to write the first honest history its people have ever held in their own hands. The book is copied, banned, recopied, argued over, and eventually taught in the same schools that once hid the truth. It does not solve every hunger, but it ends the empire's monopoly on memory.

scenes.title: Ending - Merchant Empire
scenes.body: With Bo Ren's routes and Shien's credibility, food networks become stronger than old noble monopolies ever were. Markets open under strict anti-hoarding law, river tolls are reworked, and a commerce-first commonwealth quietly replaces aristocratic famine games. It is practical, prosperous, and always in danger of learning greed too well.

scenes.title: Ending - Last Guardian
scenes.body: Rather than rule the surface, Shien keeps watch where the empire once fed the Devourer. New governments rise above him, argue above him, and occasionally descend to ask for counsel they wish they did not need. He becomes the final guardian of the system's grave, respected by all and fully joined by none.

scenes.title: Ending - Peacekeeper of Ma Fong
scenes.body: Shien forces Yu Ren and Xuan into an uneasy but binding settlement rooted in truth, public ration oversight, and shared disarmament of the old famine machine. Nobody leaves the table wholly satisfied, which is why the peace lasts. Shuǐ Guó survives because compromise finally becomes braver than purity.

scenes.title: Ending - Guardian of Shuǐ Guó
scenes.body: By restoring the last shrine and refusing every crown, Shien becomes the acknowledged guardian of the covenant between water, people, and state. He stands above politics without abandoning it, enforcing boundaries no ruler may cross again. The title is not glamorous, which is exactly why it can be trusted.

scenes.title: Ending - Savior of Nations
scenes.body: Shien carries proof of Shuǐ Guó's cycles to neighboring kingdoms and sparks reforms beyond his homeland. What began as a warning to one realm becomes a continental reckoning over how states manufacture desperation. He saves more than one nation, though each thanks him in a different language and with different levels of resentment.

scenes.title: Ending - Legend of Shuǐ Guó
scenes.body: Shien keeps his companions alive, reconciles blood with truth, rejects the old machine, and builds a covenant that makes future famine politics far harder to resurrect. Yu Ren finds peace without vanishing from memory, the dragon returns to the sea without abandoning the land, and Shuǐ Guó learns how to survive without being terror-managed into obedience. Later generations remember many titles for Shien Yu, but one endures above the rest: the legend who ended hunger's throne.
